items.title: Even kids want gambling ads banned, so why won't the government do it?

children.content: The government's proposed crackdown on gambling ads doesn't go as far as most Australians want. When you run the numbers, it's a win for the betting industry.

image.altText: Hands holding a mobile phone displaying a gambling app

items.title: Canberra man facing almost 70 sexual assault, violence charges

children.content: Police said multiple women contacted them in late 2025 and early 2026 to accuse the man of sexual assault while he was in relationships with them.

image.altText: A man with his hands cuffed behind his back is led away by a police officer.

items.title: Cost of ANU's reputational damage 'in the order of $100 million'

children.content: The interim vice-chancellor of the Australian National University says a string of governance problems has cost the university about $100 million in reputational damage.

Big sign saying Australian National University.

Big sign saying Australian National University.

items.title: 80yo to plead guilty over Canberra car crash that killed 4yo boy

children.content: Police allege the woman was driving a blue hatchback when it hit the child before continuing into a shopfront at the Southlands Shopping Centre in Mawson in January this year.

image.altText: A car crashed into a shopping centre with police tape.

items.title: Brumbies set unwanted records as winless Aussie finals run continues

children.content: The ACT Brumbies have been dumped out of the Super Rugby Pacific finals series after setting unwanted club records in Wellington on Friday.

image.altText: Rugby players with hands on hips, looking dejected

items.title: Canberra Liberals say alleged violent threat was a 'misunderstanding'

children.content: The Canberra Liberals have distanced themselves from former leader Leanne Castley's allegation that she was threatened with physical assault, saying the remark related to an "animated discussion" that was quickly resolved.

gallery.altText: Two politicians speak at a press conference.

thumbnail.altText: Two politicians speak at a press conference.

items.title: ANU approved $250m cost-cutting plan without clear evidence, report finds

children.content: Australian National University's leadership approved a controversial $250 million cost-cutting program without a clear understanding of the problem it was trying to solve or the risks involved, a report has found.

image.altText: Signage for the ANU.

items.title: Rebecca Vassarotti returns to ACT politics after Shane Rattenbury's exit

children.content: Rebecca Vassarotti has been re-elected to the ACT Legislative Assembly in an electoral countback after the resignation of ACT Greens leader Shane Rattenbury.

image.altText: A woman with short brown hair wearing a green jacket smiles, standing behind microphones.

items.title: A US ally is getting nuclear-powered submarines with no AUKUS. Here's how

children.content: South Korea is taking a different path to Australia in building nuclear-powered submarines, but its plan still hinges on the United States.

items.title: Chalmers told tax changes threaten next wave of medical breakthroughs

children.content: Medical technology companies say new restrictions on tax refunds for research and development will hurt health startups in Australia.

items.title: Chris Minns has a no-frills plan to stop One Nation in NSW. Will it work?

children.content: NSW Premier Chris Minns is enjoying growing support in his electorate, but is still concerned about Pauline Hanson's presence in next year's state election.

items.title: Could a 'mansion tax' help the housing crisis?

children.content: Amid some of the most drastic proposed changes to Australia's tax system in decades and years of skyrocketing home values, one leading expert says it is an idea "worth considering".

articles.title: Home owners told no need for 'panicking' amid Sydney property slump

children.content: Sydney property prices have dipped 0.9 per cent in the past month, following a tepid six months. But how worried should home owners be?

items.title: White House to accelerate development of AI for 'war fighting'

children.content: As a major AI company warns of an increased risk of "humans losing control over AI systems", the Trump administration says it will accelerate the development and use of artificial intelligence for national security applications.

items.title: The 20-minute coffee van that keeps a small community together

children.content: Residents of the tiny town of Underbool, in Victoria's remote Mallee region, flock to this small coffee van, which has even smaller opening hours.

items.title: Acid attack victim's lawyer describes trial of perpetrators as a 'sham'

children.content: Four military officers are facing trial over an acid attack on an Indonesian human rights campaigner that left him seriously disfigured. But some lawyers say the legal process is a "sham".

image.altText: Andrie Yunus Profile

items.title: Andrew made money subletting cottages on his almost rent-free estate, watchdog finds

children.content: Former prince Andrew pocketed income from cottages on the Windsor estate while living at Royal Lodge under a lease that required virtually no rent, a watchdog report has found.

image.altText: Andrew seen through the window of a car, with a red, purplish mark on his cheek.

items.title: How Matt Corby overcame 19 years of fear and found magic in tragedy

children.content: He got his start as a singing TV idol, but Matt Corby is making music for himself now, finding the magic in grief, growth and family.

items.title: The club I wanted to join was waiting in Pompeii

children.content: A newly uncovered fresco in Pompeii tells the story of women, wine and Roman hedonism. Buried beneath the spectacle lies a lesson about restraint and why some discoveries should be left to the future.

image.altText: painted murals on a wall in an excavated building in Pompeii

items.title: Artists confront the indignities of renting and intergenerational warfare

children.content: Zoe Pepper and Fiona Wright are among the millennial artists using their work to tackle the housing crisis, which has seen house prices rise by 400 per cent in 25 years.

items.title: 'Very troubled project': Hospital cost blowout concerns treasurer

items.title: Ponga injured in Knights' loss ahead of Origin II

items.title: Tap-and-go payments introduced to myki technology on Victorian train network

children.content: Melbourne commuters will be able to use their credit card, phone or smartwatch across the train network this month, joining other major cities with ticketless travel after repeated delays.

items.title: Residents say public housing works have blocked them from their own yards

items.title: PM admits One Nation's rise influenced decision to overhaul tax system

items.title: 'One of a kind' former senator, Catholic priest Michael Tate dies aged 80

children.content: Former Tasmanian senator and Catholic priest Michael Tate, who made the move from politics to the priesthood, has been remembered as "someone who gives himself to his people".

items.title: Ecologist worried damaged boat could pollute sensitive rock platform

items.title: Zverev books French Open final berth as Cobolli advances via walkover

children.content: Alexander Zverev moves to within touching distance of a long-coveted major title, while Flavio Cobolli reaches the final after Matteo Arnaldi withdraws due to illness.

items.title: Couple back at Finke Desert Race after finding love via social post

children.content: When motorbike enthusiast Ben Rawcliffe posted on a Finke Desert Race Facebook group looking for his future wife, his post garnered hundreds of comments. Now, he's back in the red centre, supporting his new partner as she takes to the tough off-road track.
